title: Rhode Island couple unexpectedly become parents while on vacation in Florida
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Lauren Siwinski and Gary Glenn were surprised to learn they were expecting while vacationing in Florida.
Siwinski experienced severe abdominal pain before discovering she was in labor at a Tampa Bay hospital.
The couple named their newborn son Sebastian; jokingly nicknamed 'Cruise' by family members.

title: Juvenile humpback whale washes ashore in Winthrop
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A 30-foot-long, 35,000-pound juvenile humpback whale was found under a private dock in Winthrop, prompting a marine investigation.
Photographs of the whale were sent to the Gulf of Maine Humpback Whale Catalog for identification, and a necropsy is planned.
Since 2016, the East Coast has seen 'unusual mortality events' involving humpbacks, with entanglement, strikes, and disease as key causes.

title: Jahmai Jones hits memorable first home run for Red Sox
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Jahmai Jones hit his first home run for the Red Sox in his second at-bat, tying the game against the Rays.
Jones' two-run homer cleared Fenway Park's Green Monster, marking a positive start after joining Boston from the Tigers.
Jones, previously batting .135 with Detroit, hopes to regain form after batting .287 in 2025; all career homers are against lefties.

title: Wilyer Abreu joins elite Red Sox company following his back-to-back multi-HR games
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Wilyer Abreu hit back-to-back multi-home run games against the Tampa Bay Rays at Fenway Park.
Abreu is the first Red Sox player since Mookie Betts in 2016 to achieve consecutive multi-home run games.
Boston Red Sox moved above .500 this season following their win against the Rays on July 18, 2026.

title: Red Sox extend season-altering win streak to 12 games in epic comeback fashion
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Boston Red Sox defeated the Tampa Bay Rays to extend their win streak to 12 games, their longest since 2006.
Wilyer Abreu hit a crucial home run in the seventh inning, marking his second consecutive multi-home run game.
Closer Aroldis Chapman secured the save despite a shaky ninth inning, maintaining Boston's position as a wild-card contender.

title: Report: LeBron James ‘hates’ Celtics, would rank them last on his list of teams to join
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: LeBron James reportedly ranks the Boston Celtics last on his list of potential teams to join.
ESPN's Brian Windhorst states LeBron James "hates" the Celtics due to past rivalries and mistreatment by fans.
James is considering the Heat, Cavaliers, and Warriors as possible teams for his next NBA season.

title: Tom Brady busy at Fanatics Fest, from a face slap to showing off his arm
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Tom Brady slapped Logan Paul during a panel at Fanatics Fest in New York City on July 17, 2026.
The slap followed a flag football feud; Brady's and Paul's teams clashed in a tournament earlier in 2026.
Brady also showcased his throwing arm and participated in various sports activities at Fanatics Fest.

title: 4 charged in connection with alleged kidnapping, beating near Nantucket park
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Four Nantucket men face charges for allegedly kidnapping and beating a 28-year-old near Winter Park.
Police identified suspects as Calray Brown, Dijon Hazel, Newton Lawrence, and Alvin Barnett, all from Nantucket.
Victim was tied up with an extension cord and suffered injuries, treated at the scene then hospitalized.

title: 2 arrested at Logan Airport as ICE activity ramps up
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Maria Rosales and David Ardila were arrested at Logan Airport by ICE during targeted operations.
Rosales, a DACA recipient with a pending renewal, was detained due to a 2017 removal order.
Ardila, a U.S. asylum seeker, was arrested for allegedly overstaying his visa pending application decision.

title: 5 movies to watch after ‘The Odyssey’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Interstellar' (2014) mirrors 'The Odyssey' through its sci-fi narrative featuring time travel and a quest for family reunion.
'O Brother, Where Art Thou?' (2000) humorously adapts Homer's epic with characters resembling Odysseus and mythological elements.
'The Searchers' (1956), a classic Western, parallels 'The Odyssey' with a protagonist's extended quest to rescue his kidnapped niece.
